cards.title: Consider parents caring for adult children not in work
cards.body: One reader was surprised by how many people equate successful parenting with independence. The discussion reveals diverse perspectives on adult children and family expectations.

cards.title: I wish my son wanted to spend more time with me
cards.body: A parent seeks advice about their adult son's limited contact despite weekly touch-ins. The columnist explores whether parental pressure might be affecting the relationship.

cards.title: Having children makes you smarter? Incredible, but true
cards.body: Recent studies show mothers and fathers have healthier, younger-looking brains. Years of mental and emotional parenting gymnastics may actually boost cognitive function.

cards.title: Family holiday worry: learning to cope with real emergencies
cards.body: A parent struggled with anxiety during vacation but discovered resilience when facing an actual crisis. The experience shifted her understanding of worry and parental capability.

cards.title: The real reason behind kids' attention-seeking behavior
cards.body: Setting firmer boundaries rarely works when children constantly demand attention. Understanding the underlying needs is more effective than stricter discipline.

cards.title: Raising decision-makers in a high-stakes world
cards.body: Brains aren't fully developed until age 25, yet kids face major decisions earlier. Parents can help guide good choices by understanding adolescent brain development.

cards.title: Parents warned not to publicly share children's images
cards.body: The NCA warns of growing threats to children whose images are shared online. AI abuse risks mean parents should reconsider posting kids' photos publicly.

cards.title: Parents criticise 'inappropriate' performer at youth music festival
cards.body: A musician's set at a youth festival included sexual questions inappropriate for young children. Parents voiced concerns about age-appropriate content at family events.
